text: How we spend our days is, of course, how we spend our lives.
my_thought: A reminder that there is no someday. Days are the unit of a life.
Most of my suffering is timeshare in tomorrow.

text: I have always imagined that Paradise will be a kind of library.
my_thought: Heaven as a room of books. The reading is the afterlife.

text: No man ever steps in the same river twice, for it's not the same river and he's not the same man.
my_thought: Identity as flow, not substance. The river and the man, co-evolving.

text: Every day is a journey, and the journey itself is home.

source.title: Narrow Road to the Deep North
source.author: Matsuo Bashō

my_thought: Home isn't where you settle. It's the moving.
